steps.title: Identify the shared premise about the designation's impact
steps.content:
Text 1 presents what happened in Rasht: the designation brought awareness and a tourism boost.

Text 2 acknowledges the same potential but explains that realizing full benefits requires intentional marketing strategy.

The common ground is that both texts address whether the designation can create positive impact on the food scene and tourism.

steps.title: Find what both authors would agree on
steps.content:
The correct answer must express common ground both authors would accept. Choice C — “A city's food scene can benefit from the city being named a City of Gastronomy” — does this through the word “can” (potential).

Text 1 author confirms it: Rasht's food scene benefited with awareness and tourism boost. Text 2 author implies it: cities can achieve benefits if they implement proper marketing strategies.

The other choices either introduce non-tourism benefits not discussed in both texts, directly contradict Text 1 (which states the designation is well-known to visitors), or make a universal claim about guarantees that Text 1's confident tone about Rasht doesn't endorse.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
